title: Program Browsing
steps:
1. Navigate to Home tab
2. Tap on 'Programs' section or navigate via bottom nav
3. Browse programs by category (Strength, Hypertrophy, Body Recomp, Performance)
4. Tap on a program card to view details
5. Verify program info: description, duration, number of workouts, difficulty level
expected:
- Programs load and display correctly
- Categories filter programs properly
- Program details show: title, description, workout count, duration
- Progress bar shows 'X/Y workouts complete' if enrolled
- Premium programs show lock indicator for non-subscribers
notes: Route: /programs -> ProgramsScreen

title: Free Program Enrollment
preconditions: User is logged in, program is free (not premium)
steps:
1. Navigate to Programs screen
2. Select a free (non-premium) workout program
3. Tap 'Start Program' button
4. Verify enrollment confirmation
5. Check program appears with progress tracking
6. Navigate to My Activities to verify scheduled workouts
expected:
- Enrollment completes without paywall
- Program shows as enrolled with 0/X progress
- Scheduled activity logs created for all program workouts
- User can access program workouts immediately
notes: Enrollment calls ProgramCubit.subscribeToProgram(). Verify no SubscribeToProgramDialog appears for free programs.

title: Paid Program Enrollment (subscription required)
testData: Test with: (A) Subscribed account, (B) Free account
steps:
Scenario A - Subscribed User:
1. Login with subscribed account
2. Navigate to a premium program
3. Tap 'Start Program'
4. Verify enrollment succeeds

Scenario B - Free User:
1. Login with free account
2. Navigate to a premium program
3. Tap 'Start Program'
4. Verify SubscribeToProgramDialog appears
5. Verify paywall/subscription options shown
expected:
Scenario A: Program unlocks and enrolls successfully
Scenario B: SubscribeToProgramDialog shown with subscription options, cannot proceed without subscription
notes: Revenue impact - P0. Dialog component: SubscribeToProgramDialog

title: Workout Session Initiation
preconditions: User enrolled in a program OR has access to standalone workout
steps:
1. Navigate to enrolled program or workout
2. Tap 'Start Workout' button
3. If existing active/paused workout: BBStartNewWorkoutDialog appears - choose action
4. Verify WorkoutTimerComponent loads
5. Check exercise list displays correctly
6. Verify timer starts
7. Check set/rep/weight inputs are visible
expected:
- Smooth navigation to ActiveWorkoutScreen
- Exercise list loads with all movements
- Each movement shows: name, sets, default reps/weight
- Timer visible and running
- Can swipe between exercises
- Progress indicator shows current position
notes: Routes: /activeWorkoutScreen or /fullScreenWorkoutScreen (for video workouts). Core component: WorkoutTimerComponent. State: ActivityLoggingCubit.logActivityStart()

title: Workout Session Completion
steps:
1. Complete all exercises (mark sets as done)
2. Tap 'Finish Workout' button
3. Verify completion confirmation
4. Check WorkoutResultsComponent displays
5. Verify workout summary: total time, exercises completed, volume
6. Navigate to My Activities
7. Verify workout appears in history
expected:
- Workout saved to local DB immediately
- Background sync to server
- WorkoutResultsComponent shows: duration, movements completed, total volume
- Workout logged to My Activities with correct date/time
- Program progress updated (X/Y workouts)
notes: Calls ActivityLoggingCubit.logCompleteWorkout(). Data persists locally first, then syncs via ActivityLogSyncEngine. Route: /workoutResults

title: Log Sets, Reps, Weight
steps:
1. During workout, navigate to an exercise
2. Tap +/- buttons to adjust reps for Set 1
3. Tap +/- buttons to adjust weight
4. Mark set as complete (tap checkmark or complete button)
5. Verify rest timer starts
6. Repeat for Sets 2 and 3 with different values
7. Verify all values saved
expected:
- +/- buttons increment/decrement values smoothly
- Values update immediately in UI
- Set completion triggers rest timer
- Values persist (debounced 1 second save)
- Weight converts properly (imperial to metric for storage)
- Can view previous set values
notes: Uses WorkoutMovementCubit: incrementOrDecrementReps(), incrementOrDecrementWeight(). Values stored as kg in DB (_toServerWeight converts). Edge cases: negative numbers blocked, very large numbers.

title: Edit Logged Values
preconditions: Active workout with at least one completed set
steps:
1. Complete a set with specific values
2. Return to the completed set
3. Tap on reps/weight field or use +/- buttons
4. Modify the values
5. Verify changes save
6. Complete workout and check history shows updated values
expected:
- Can edit previously logged values
- Changes save automatically (1 second debounce)
- History reflects updated values
- No data corruption
notes: Uses WorkoutMovementCubit.setValueInSet() for direct edits. Debounced via ActivityLogSessionService.updateSet()

title: Delete Logged Sets
preconditions: Active workout with multiple sets logged
steps:
1. Log multiple sets for an exercise
2. Find delete/remove option for a set
3. Delete a set
4. Verify set is removed from list
5. Verify remaining sets re-ordered correctly
6. Complete workout and verify history is accurate
expected:
- Set removed from exercise
- Remaining sets maintain correct order
- Total set count updates
- Deleted set not included in workout history
notes: Uses WorkoutMovementCubit.removeSet(index). Also can addSet() for extra sets beyond template.

title: App Backgrounding During Workout
preconditions: Active workout session with logged data
steps:
1. Start workout, log 2-3 sets
2. Press home button to background app
3. Wait 2 minutes
4. Return to app
5. Verify workout state
6. Wait 30+ minutes, return again
7. Verify data still intact
expected:
- App resumes to workout screen
- All logged data preserved
- Timer paused or continues correctly
- Can continue workout normally
- Long backgrounding (30+ min) still preserves state
notes: Uses ActivityLoggingCubit.logActivityPause() on background, logActivityResume() on return. Data stored in local Drift DB. Critical for user experience.

title: Force Close During Workout
preconditions: Active workout session with logged data
steps:
1. Start workout, log several sets
2. Force quit app (swipe up from app switcher)
3. Reopen app
4. Check for recovery prompt or active workout indicator
5. Navigate to My Activities
6. Check for in-progress or paused workout
expected:
- App reopens normally
- Workout appears as paused/in-progress in My Activities
- Can tap to resume workout
- All previously logged data intact
- Uses pausedActivityLogId to resume
notes: Data persists via local DB. Resume flow: MyActivitiesScreen -> tap paused workout -> ActiveWorkoutScreen with pausedActivityLogId. Verify dirty flag triggers sync on resume.

title: Network Loss During Workout
steps:
1. Start workout with network on
2. Log a few sets
3. Enable airplane mode
4. Continue logging sets
5. Complete workout
6. Verify completion saves locally
7. Disable airplane mode
8. Verify data syncs to server
expected:
- Workout continues without interruption offline
- All data saves to local Drift DB
- Completion works offline
- ActivityLogSyncEngine syncs when back online
- No data loss
notes: ActivityLogSyncEngine runs on 8-second timer, processes up to 8 logs per batch. Connectivity listener triggers immediate sync when online. Logs marked 'dirty' until synced.

title: Low Battery / Phone Call During Workout
steps:
1. Start workout, log some sets
2. Receive phone call (or simulate with test call)
3. Answer call, stay on for 1-2 minutes
4. End call, return to app
5. Verify workout state
6. Test low battery warning (if possible to trigger)
expected:
- Workout state preserved during interruption
- Timer paused appropriately
- All data intact after returning
- Can continue workout normally
notes: Same mechanism as backgrounding - pause/resume state management. Lower priority but important for user experience.

title: Create Custom Workout
steps:
1. Navigate to My Activities
2. Tap 'Create Workout' or '+' button
3. Enter workout name and description
4. Tap 'Add Movement' / 'Add Exercises'
5. Search and select exercises (2-3 exercises)
6. For each exercise: set number of sets, reps, weight
7. Optionally create a superset (group exercises)
8. Reorder exercises via drag
9. Set rest timers between sets
10. Save workout
11. Verify workout appears in saved workouts
12. Start the custom workout
expected:
- Can name and describe workout
- Exercise search works
- Can add multiple exercises
- Can configure sets/reps/weight per exercise
- Supersets group exercises together
- Drag to reorder works
- Rest timer configurable
- Workout saves and is accessible later
- Can start and log the custom workout
notes: FEATURE EXISTS! Route: /createWorkout -> CreateWorkoutScreen. Uses CreateWorkoutCubit with methods: addNewMovement(), createOrAddToSuperset(), updateSet(), reorderMovements(). Auto-saves to server.

title: HealthKit Data Sync
preconditions: iOS device with HealthKit enabled, permissions granted
steps:
1. Ensure HealthKit permissions granted for BB.com app
2. Complete a workout in the app
3. Open Apple Health app
4. Check Workouts section
5. Verify BB.com workout appears
6. Check data: duration, calories (if tracked)

Reverse sync test:
7. Complete workout in another app (Apple Fitness, etc.)
8. Open BB.com app
9. Check if workout imported from HealthKit
expected:
- Workout exports to HealthKit
- Duration and calories visible in Apple Health
- Can import workouts from HealthKit into BB.com
- Heart rate, steps, distance synced if available
notes: FEATURE EXISTS! Uses HealthCubit with health package. Tracks: STEPS, HEART_RATE, WORKOUT, DISTANCE_WALKING_RUNNING, WEIGHT, ACTIVE_ENERGY_BURNED. Import via generateWorkoutData(). Android uses Google Health Connect.

title: Very Long Workout Session
steps:
1. Start a workout
2. Log sets periodically over 2+ hours
3. Include breaks/pauses
4. Complete workout
5. Verify all data saved correctly
6. Check timer shows correct total duration
7. Verify no memory issues or app slowdown
expected:
- App remains stable over extended period
- All data logged correctly
- Timer accurate
- No memory leaks
- Sync engine continues working
- Battery usage reasonable
notes: Tests app stability and memory management. Sync engine runs every 8 seconds - verify no buildup of unsent data.

title: Rapid Set Logging
steps:
1. During workout, rapidly tap +/- buttons
2. Quickly complete multiple sets in succession
3. Spam the complete set button
4. Navigate quickly between exercises
5. Verify all data recorded correctly
expected:
- UI remains responsive
- No duplicate entries
- All taps register correctly
- Debouncing prevents API spam
- Data integrity maintained
notes: Tests UI performance and debouncing. WorkoutMovementCubit has 1-second debounce on updates. Verify no race conditions.

title: Missing Exercise Data
preconditions: Access to workout with potentially missing exercise data
testData: May need backend manipulation or specific test workout
steps:
1. Attempt to start workout with exercise that has missing data
2. Observe app behavior
3. Check for error handling / fallbacks
expected:
- App handles gracefully without crash
- Error message or placeholder shown
- User can skip or workout continues
- No data corruption for other exercises
notes: Lower priority edge case. Difficult to reproduce without backend access. Consider skipping or marking N/A if cannot be tested.

title: Device Lock During Workout
preconditions: Passcode/FaceID enabled on device, active workout
steps:
1. Start workout, log a set
2. Let device auto-lock (or manually lock)
3. Wait 1-2 minutes
4. Unlock device
5. Return to app
6. Verify workout state preserved
expected:
- Workout state preserved
- Data intact
- Timer behavior appropriate (paused or continued based on design)
- Can continue workout normally
notes: Similar to backgrounding test. Verifies pause/resume works with device lock.

title: Multiple Workouts Same Day
steps:
1. Complete first workout in the morning
2. Verify it saves to My Activities
3. Start and complete second workout later same day
4. Navigate to My Activities
5. Verify both workouts appear separately
6. Check dates/times are correct
7. Verify no data conflicts or overwrites
expected:
- Both workouts saved as separate entries
- Correct timestamps for each
- No data conflicts
- Program progress counts both if applicable
- History shows accurate data for each
notes: Tests activity log uniqueness. Each workout gets unique activityLogId. Verify BBStartNewWorkoutDialog appears if starting second while first is active.

title: Incomplete Workout - Exit Mid-Session
steps:
1. Start workout
2. Log 1-2 sets
3. Exit workout without completing (back button or navigate away)
4. Confirm exit if prompted
5. Check My Activities for workout status
6. Attempt to resume the workout
7. Verify logged data is preserved
expected:
- Workout saved as paused/in-progress
- Logged sets preserved
- Can resume workout later
- BBStartNewWorkoutDialog appears if starting new workout while incomplete exists
- Option to abandon or continue
notes: Tests partial workout handling. Status becomes 'paused'. Resume via pausedActivityLogId. Verify ActivityLoggingCubit.logActivityPause() called on exit.

title: Workout with Video/Full Screen Mode
testData: Use workout that has video tutorials/guides
steps:
1. Find a workout with video content
2. Start workout in full-screen mode
3. Verify video plays correctly
4. Verify can still log sets during video
5. Test pause/play video controls
6. Complete workout
7. Verify data saved correctly
expected:
- FullScreenWorkoutScreen loads correctly
- Video plays without buffering issues
- Can interact with set logging while video plays
- Video controls work (play/pause)
- Workout completion saves all data
notes: NEW TEST CASE - Route: /fullScreenWorkoutScreen. Uses FullScreenWorkoutScreen component. Tests video workout mode distinct from standard workout.

title: Cancel Program Subscription
steps:
1. Navigate to enrolled program
2. Find cancel/unsubscribe option
3. Tap to cancel program subscription
4. Confirm cancellation in SubscriptionCancelDialog
5. Verify program shows as not enrolled
6. Check scheduled workouts removed from My Activities
expected:
- SubscriptionCancelDialog appears with confirmation
- Program subscription cancelled
- Progress cleared
- Scheduled activity logs removed
- Can re-enroll later if desired
notes: NEW TEST CASE - Tests ProgramCubit.cancelProgramSubscription(). Important for user control over enrolled programs.

title: Resume Paused Workout
preconditions: Paused workout exists in My Activities
steps:
1. Ensure a paused workout exists (from previous session)
2. Navigate to My Activities
3. Find paused workout (should show 'paused' or 'in progress' status)
4. Tap on the paused workout
5. Verify resume prompt or automatic resume
6. Verify all previous data loaded
7. Continue workout and complete
expected:
- Paused workout visible in My Activities
- Tapping resumes workout
- All previously logged sets preserved
- Timer continues from paused state
- Can complete workout normally
notes: NEW TEST CASE - Critical user flow. Uses pausedActivityLogId parameter. Calls ActivityLoggingCubit.logActivityResume().

title: Superset Exercise Logging
testData: Custom workout with superset or program workout with supersets
steps:
1. Start workout containing a superset
2. Navigate to superset exercises
3. Verify superset grouping visible in UI
4. Log set for first exercise in superset
5. Immediately log set for second exercise
6. Verify both tracked together
7. Complete all superset rounds
8. Verify data saved correctly
expected:
- Superset exercises grouped visually
- Can log alternating sets between exercises
- All movements in superset have same number of sets
- Data saves correctly for all superset exercises
notes: NEW TEST CASE - Tests CreateWorkoutCubit.createOrAddToSuperset() functionality. Supersets require special UI handling.
